name: Ajanta Caves
description: Ancient Buddhist rock-cut cave monuments dating back to the 2nd century BCE. Famous for spectacular murals and sculptures.

name: Ellora Caves
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring 34 rock-cut caves representing Hindu Buddhist and Jain monuments from 600-1000 CE.

name: Bibi Ka Maqbara
description: Known as the Taj of the Deccan this beautiful 17th-century Mughal mausoleum was built by Prince Azam Shah.

name: Daulatabad Fort
description: One of the strongest forts in India's history built on a conical hill with a 180-degree moat and labyrinthine passages.

name: Panchakki Water Mill
description: A scenic 17th-century water mill powered by an underground stream. Part of the Sufi shrine complex with beautiful gardens.

name: Grishneshwar Temple
description: One of the 12 revered Jyotirlinga shrines of Lord Shiva in India. An important pilgrimage destination with ancient architecture.

name: Salim Ali Lake Bird Sanctuary
description: Peaceful lake and bird sanctuary named after famous ornithologist. Perfect for bird watching and nature walks.

name: Siddharth Garden & Zoo
description: Large garden with zoo toy train and boating facilities. Great for family outings.

name: Aurangabad Caves
description: 12 rock-cut Buddhist shrines dating from 3rd to 7th century. Less crowded alternative to Ajanta-Ellora.

name: Khuldabad Valley of Saints
description: Historic town with tombs of Mughal emperor Aurangzeb and Sufi saints. Peaceful spiritual atmosphere.

name: Bhadra Maruti Temple
description: Unique temple with reclining Hanuman idol. One of the few temples where Hanuman is in sleeping posture.

name: Himayat Bagh
description: Historic Mughal garden with beautiful landscaping. Perfect for morning walks and picnics.
